evidence_slots.impact: A requester with reglk access can clear or weaken register locks before corresponding lock controls are asserted; unlock/reset paths clear lock vector and may defeat lifecycle stickiness.

evidence_slots.state_condition: we_flag can be active during normal operation unless externally constrained; no top-level reset/testmode qualification is visible. acct force affects acc_ctrl_o combinationally independent of acct_mem writes.
evidence_slots.impact: External flag source may bypass or modify normal permission/lock checks, granting access or altering protected debug/DMA/AES behavior.

rtl_evidence.evidence: top-level inputs we_flag_0 through we_flag_4
dmi_jtag receives we_flag_4
AES0 reglk_ctrl_i is reglk_ctrl slice OR we_flag_1
acct_wrapper receives we_flag_0
dma_wrapper receives we_flag_3

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Exact uses of we_flag_3 inside dma_wrapper and we_flag_4 inside dmi_jtag are not visible; no evidence was provided for we_flag_2 use beyond top-level input.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: External we_flag inputs can force or alter protected control behavior without visible testmode/authorization qualification: we_flag_0 forces acc_ctrl outputs, we_flag_1 ORs AES0 reglk_ctrl_i, we_flag_3 enters DMA wrapper, and we_flag_4 enters dmi_jtag.
inspection_records.notes: The acct_wrapper we_flag behavior alone is enough for candidate bypass of policy bits.

evidence_slots.subject: Software/NOC requester reaching rst_wrapper and external/global reset sources
evidence_slots.operation: Assert reset or clear protected state
evidence_slots.object: reglk_ctrl/reglk_mem, acc_ctrl/acct_mem, DMA config/status, pkt fuse_req/fuse_addr and peripheral state affected by rst_1..rst_13
evidence_slots.expected_guard: Software/external resets of security wrappers should require privileged lifecycle authorization and should not clear locks/policy or leave unsafe stale/observable state
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: Top-level rst_wrapper is NOC-accessible through rst_axilite_bridge and receives acct_ctrl_i(acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][12]), reglk_ctrl_i(reglk_ctrl[12]), and debug_mode_i. It outputs rst_1..rst_13 to wrappers. reglk_wrapper clears all reglk_mem on rst_9; acct_wrapper resets acct_mem to 32'hffffffff on rst_6; dma_wrapper clears config registers on rst_8; pkt_wrapper clears fuse_req_o/fuse_addr_o on rst_5. Because acct reset default is all ones and reglk reset default is zero, reset can re-open policy/clear locks if rst_wrapper access is not strictly authorized.
evidence_slots.path: rst NOC -> rst_axilite_bridge -> rst_wrapper -> rst_5/rst_6/rst_8/rst_9 -> pkt/acct/dma/reglk sequential reset branches
evidence_slots.state_condition: Local resets differ from global rst_ni and are independently routed. Reset effects include lock clear to zero, access-control default to all ones, DMA config clear, and fuse request/address clear. Reset wrapper access itself depends on potentially default-open acc_ctrl.
evidence_slots.impact: Authorized-by-default or lower-privileged reset access can clear locks/policy and return system to permissive state, undermining lifecycle protection and residue guarantees.

rst_6/reset sets acct_mem to 32'hffffffff
rst_8/reset clears DMA config registers
rst_5/reset clears fuse_req_o and fuse_addr_o

inspection_records.candidate_finding: Software/NOC-accessible reset wrapper can assert per-peripheral resets that clear security-relevant state; reset control is only gated by acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][12]/reglk_ctrl and debug_mode_i, while downstream resets clear locks, access policy, DMA config, and fuse controls.
inspection_records.notes: The reset path composes with default-open acct reset and non-sticky reglk reset, increasing severity.
